mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals intact consumer spending that drives corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of non-overlapping months when RSAFS is above its 12-month trailing average.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25% signals constrained monetary policy space that elevates equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and increases in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicate a deteriorating labor market that reduces household income and equity cash flow expectations, jointly forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods when PAYEMS decreases and UNRATE increases simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) predicts positive S&P 500 returns when housing activity is healthy, operationalized as housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 800 thousand units, indicating broad-based supply-side expansion.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 800, positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 900 thousand units signal strong construction demand and durable goods spillovers that forecast positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when HOUST exceeds 900 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) coinciding with a low but rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicate a reactive monetary tightening cycle that surprises markets and compresses equity valuations, jointly forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods when CPIAUCSL rises and FEDFUNDS increases from below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing utilization and corporate revenue, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when INDPRO increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 6.5% signals excess labor slack that suppresses wage growth and consumer spending, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of periods when UNRATE exceeds 6.5%.
